doctor.name: Dr. Arjun Mehta
doctor.title: Senior Consultant Cardiologist
doctor.about: Dr. Arjun Mehta is a board-certified cardiologist with over 18 years of clinical experience in diagnosing and managing complex cardiovascular conditions. He completed his MBBS from AIIMS Delhi and pursued super-specialisation in Cardiology from PGIMER Chandigarh. Dr. Mehta has authored 30+ peer-reviewed publications and is a recipient of the Young Cardiologist Award (2019). He is committed to delivering compassionate, evidence-based cardiac care with a focus on preventive cardiology and lifestyle intervention.

services.title: Cardiac Risk Assessment
services.description: Comprehensive evaluation of cardiovascular risk factors including lipid profiling, stress testing, and advanced imaging for early detection.

services.title: General Cardiology OPD
services.description: Routine cardiac check-ups, ECG interpretation, Holter monitoring, and management of hypertension, diabetes, and dyslipidaemia.

services.title: Echocardiography & Imaging
services.description: 2D Echo, Doppler studies, Stress Echo, and TEE performed and interpreted by the consultant in-clinic.

services.title: Interventional Cardiology
services.description: Coronary angiography, angioplasty with stent placement, and peripheral vascular interventions performed at partner hospitals.

services.title: Pulmonary Hypertension Clinic
services.description: Dedicated evaluation and management of pulmonary arterial hypertension with advanced hemodynamic monitoring.

services.title: Preventive Cardiology & Wellness
services.description: Personalised lifestyle modification plans, diet counselling, and cardiac rehabilitation programmes for long-term heart health.

articles.title: Understanding Hypertension: The Silent Epidemic
articles.excerpt: Nearly 1 in 3 Indian adults suffers from high blood pressure. Learn about the causes, complications, and evidence-based management strategies.

articles.title: 5 Heart-Healthy Foods You Must Include in Your Diet
articles.excerpt: Simple dietary changes can reduce cardiovascular risk by up to 40%. A cardiologist's guide to incorporating omega-3s, fibre, and antioxidants.

articles.title: When Should You Get a Cardiac Stress Test?
articles.excerpt: Stress testing is not for everyone. Explore the latest ACC/AHA guidelines on appropriate patient selection for exercise and pharmacological stress testing.

articles.title: Managing Diabetes to Protect Your Heart
articles.excerpt: Cardiovascular disease is the leading cause of morbidity in diabetics. A practical framework for glycaemic control and lipid management.
